english: lar (lar) Sch. - *gár-te bór-ċe* (*gar te bor ce*) W. to set apart, exclude, shut out; to lock up, shut up, to lay up or by, to preserve; *gár-gya ċó-ċe* (*gar rgya co ce*) W. to store up; *tób-ċi gár-ċe* (*thob ci gar ce*) to button up. - 2. to hang up, to fasten, to attach, *dhar-ċóg túg-pa-la* (*dhar cog tug pa la*) C. a flag to a rope. Cf. skár-ba (skar ba).

1. nine, dgú-bcu (dgu bcu) (tám-pa (tham pa)) ninety; dgú-bcu (dgu bcu) rtsa gċíg (rtsa gcig), or go-ċíg (go cig), W. *gu-bcu-go-ċíg* (*gu bcu go cig*) ninety one etc.; dgú-pa (dgu pa) 1. the ninth. 2. having, comprising, measuring, nine, e.g. kru-dgú-pa (khru dgu pa) measuring nine cubits (in length, height etc.); dgú-po (dgu po) the nine, those nine; lan-dgú (lan dgu) nine times; dgu-ñíṅ (dgu rnying) three years ago col. - 2. many, dgú-cig (dgu cig) id. Mil.; tabs dgus (thabs dgus) bsags (bsags), gathered by many efforts, with great difficulty; used as sign of the plural: skyé-dgu (skye dgu) men, skye-dgui (skye dgui) bdág-po (bdag po) (Ssk. प्रजापति) the lord of creatures, the lord of men; skye-dgui-bdág-mo (skye dgui bdag mo) n. of the aunt and wet-nurse of Buddha; yód-dgu (yod dgu) Lex. those that are, the existing beings; nor yód-dgu-ċòg (nor yod dgu cog) Mil. the goods that one has, property; bzáṅ-dgu (bzang dgu) Lex. the good and the brave (among men); lus dod dgur (lus dod dgur) sgyúr-ba (sgyur ba) to be changed, transformed, ad libitum Mil.; ñan-dgu (nyan dgu) túb-pa (thub pa) Lt. to overcome every evil; mi s̀es (mi shes) dgu s̀és-po (dgu shes po) Thgy. he that knows every thing; *mí ǰhé' yu ǰhé' mi yoṅ gu yoṅ* (*mi jhe' yu jhe' mi yong gu yong*) C. if you do many things which ought not to be done, many things will take place which ought not to take place; ći-ba (chi ba) yul-kyi (yul kyi) dgú-la (dgu la) mi byéd-de (mi byed de) Thgy. not counting death among things to be thought of. - 3. inst. of dgun (dgun), dgu-zla (dgu zla) winter-month Mil. frq - zer-dgu (zer dgu), smra-dgu (smra dgu)??
for tses nyer-dgui (tshes nyer dgui) ytor-ma (gtor ma), a sacrifice on the 29th day of the month W.
Mil., Thg., a particular kind of meditation.

1. vb. to bend, to make crooked; *go gú-ċe* (*go gu ce*) Ld. to bend, bow, stop; to submit. - 2. sbst. the act of bending, bowing, inflection. - 3. adj. bent, stooping; dgú-po (dgu po), dgú-mo (dgu mo) Cs. id.
n. of a yellow flower Cs.

wylie: dgu tshigs, dgu tshigs skya mo
english: the galaxy, the milky way Mil.

another form for guṅ (gung) (the former of the two appears to be prevalent) 1. the middle. - 2. noon, mid-day. - 3. mid-night. - 4. heaven. dguṅ-la (dgung la) reg (reg) it reached up to heaven Mil.; dguṅ sṅón-po (dgung sngon po) the blue heaven, yá-gi dguṅ-sṅón (ya gi dgung sngon) the blue heaven above Mil.; dgúṅ-du (dgung du) (or -la (-la)) gṡégs-pa (gshegs pa) (lit. to repair, to withdraw, to heaven) to die Mil. and elsewh. - 5. before dates, esp. before the word lo (lo), it serves as a respectful word, and is e.g. frq. used in stating the age of a Buddha or a king; yet it occurs also in compounds, where no such bearing is discernible: dguṅ-żág (dgung zhag), dguṅ-zla (dgung zla) Cs.; dguṅ-do-núb (dgung do nub) Mil. this evening, to-night; dguṅ-snyiṅ (dgung snying) a year, a year of one's life; dguṅ-kág (dgung khag) division of time (?); dguṅ-bdún (dgung bdun) a week. (Cs. has also dguṅ-tíg (dgung thig), and dguṅ-tíg-gi (dgung thig gi) dkyil-kór (dkyil khor), which terms were prob. framed by him, and meant to denote the meridian line and meridian circle.)
evening Sch, perh. a corruption of dgóṅ-mo (dgong mo).

W. *gún-ka* (*gun ka*) winter; dgún (dgun) is also used adverbially: in winter(-time), during winter; dgún-dus (dgun dus) winter-time; dgun-tóg (dgun thog), dgun-tog-tág (dgun thog thag), W. *gun-tag-tóg* (*gun tag tog*), all the winter through; *gun tse re* (*gun tse re*) W. every winter; dgun gráṅ-bai dús-na (dgun grang bai dus na) during the cold of winter Dzl.; dgun-nyi-ldog (dgun nyi ldog) the win-
